summary: Matched the common fighter HammerWalk state by moving 416 bytes of code out of asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s into a new src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerWalk.c and header. The matched file covers ftCo_800C55CC, the HammerWalk transition helper ftCo_800C5614, and the HammerWalk Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks at 100.00%. The PR also added the new C object to obj_files.mk, moved HammerWalk prototypes out of the generic ft_0C31.h placeholder header into ftCo_HammerWalk.h, and updated HammerWait/Init includes to use the new header.
what_changed: Deleted the HammerWalk assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s, including ftCo_800C55CC, ftCo_800C5614, ftCo_HammerWalk_Anim, ftCo_HammerWalk_IASA, ftCo_HammerWalk_Phys, ftCo_HammerWalk_Coll, and the local sdata2 constants previously used by that assembly region.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerWalk.c with matched C implementations for six HammerWalk-related functions totaling 416 bytes.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerWalk.h with public prototypes for ftCo_800C55CC and the HammerWalk callbacks, using address comments for the 0C55CC through 0C574C range.
Added the new HammerWalk C object to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk immediately after ftCo_HammerWait.c.o and before the remaining ft_0C31 assembly object.
Removed HammerWalk prototypes from src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h, including the previous placeholder-style UNK_RET declaration for ftCo_800C5614.
Removed ftCo_800C55CC from ftCo_HammerWait.h and included ftCo_HammerWalk.h where needed by ftCo_HammerWait.c and ftCo_Init.c.
Kept ftCo_800C5614 local to ftCo_HammerWalk.c as a static helper declaration, rather than exposing it in the new public header.
smart_moves: Split the newly matched code into a focused ftCo_HammerWalk.c/ftCo_HammerWalk.h pair rather than leaving HammerWalk declarations in the broad ft_0C31.h placeholder header.
Made ftCo_800C5614 static in the C file, reflecting that it is an internal HammerWalk transition helper and reducing unnecessary header surface area.
Preserved direct wrapper callback structure for Anim, Phys, and Coll: HammerWalk Anim delegates to ftCo_800C4F64, Phys delegates to ftWalkCommon_800E0060, and Coll delegates to ftCo_HammerWait_Coll.
Used RETURN_IF in ftCo_HammerWalk_IASA to express the original early-exit chain from assembly while keeping the control flow compact and match-friendly.
Localized the known matching constraint with a comment: the flags declaration cannot move below fp, suggesting an inline-sensitive ordering issue.
Updated include ownership so consumers include ftCo_HammerWalk.h directly, avoiding reliance on unrelated HammerWait or ft_0C31 declarations.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling a small contiguous state block, move both the code and its prototypes into a state-specific file/header and leave the remaining assembly object in place for still-unmatched neighboring functions.
A function that was globally visible in assembly can often become static in C if it is only an implementation detail of the newly decompiled translation unit; verify usage before exposing it.
Source ordering can matter for matching: this PR records that declaring u32 flags before Fighter* fp was necessary, with the comment suggesting an inline/register allocation sensitivity.
For fighter state callbacks that are simple pass-throughs, exact delegation to existing common helpers can be enough to match the assembly wrappers.
RETURN_IF is useful for matching sequential IASA checks that return immediately when a helper succeeds.
Header cleanup is part of decompilation: replace placeholder declarations in broad headers with typed declarations in feature-specific headers once a file is matched.
naming_conventions: New file and header use the ftCo_HammerWalk basename, matching the fighter common action/state naming style already used by ftCo_HammerWait.
Public state callback names follow ftCo_HammerWalk_Anim, ftCo_HammerWalk_IASA, ftCo_HammerWalk_Phys, and ftCo_HammerWalk_Coll.
Unidentified helper names remain address-based, such as ftCo_800C55CC and ftCo_800C5614, instead of inventing semantic names without evidence.
The header guard is GALE01_0C55CC, matching the address-oriented guard style used in this codebase slice.
Address comments are retained on prototypes, such as /* 0C55CC */ and /* 0C56BC */, to preserve symbol-location searchability.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a 130-line assembly region after achieving a 100.00% match for the corresponding C file.
Recreated the ftCo_800C55CC branch as an if around ftWalkCommon_800DFC70(gobj), calling ftCo_800C5614 and returning true only on success.

Matched ftCo_800C5614 by computing accel_mul from fp->x34_scale.y, conditionally calling ftCo_CalcYScaledKnockback with Fighter_804D6524 data when y scale is not 1, then storing fp->mv.co.walk.accel_mul and fp->mv.co.walk.x0.
Used a separate u32 flags local before GET_FIGHTER(gobj); the source comment says moving it below fp breaks the match and may indicate an inline.
Called Fighter_ChangeMotionState with ftCo_MS_HammerWalk, the flags from ftCo_800C54C4(fp), ftCo_800C548C(fp), 1, 0, and NULL, followed by ftAnim_8006EBA4(gobj) and ftCo_800C4E94(fp).
Expressed the IASA assembly chain as three ordered RETURN_IF calls: ftCo_800C5A50, ftCo_800C60C8, then ftCo_800C4E4C.
Moved float/NULL constants out of assembly implicitly by using C literals 1 and NULL in the matched source.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or document ftCo_800C55CC and ftCo_800C5614 with semantic names.
Search for other Hammer state PRs that reuse ftCo_HammerWait_Coll or ftWalkCommon_800E0060 for shared callback behavior.
Investigate the comment in ftCo_800C5614 about flags ordering and a possible inline to see whether later struct or inline discoveries removed the matching constraint.
Check neighboring functions in ft_0C31.s after 0C576C for subsequent HammerTurn or Hammer-related decompilation work.
